definition: The Kalecki profit equation mechanism through which government fiscal deficits directly sustain corporate sector profits. At ~6-7% GDP deficit, this channel provides a powerful floor under risk asset valuations and extends risk-on regimes.
evidence: Kalecki profit equation framework linking sectoral balances to profits
Empirical observation of profit resilience during high-deficit periods
Theoretical mechanism for fiscal-to-corporate profit transmission
S&P 500 EPS growth of 8-10% through 2024-25 consistent with Kalecki attribution
Food volumes declined only 2-3% during 40-year-high food inflation in 2021-22 vs 8-12% in 2008 without fiscal transfers
HY default rates suppressed at ~2.5% vs historical 4.1% base rate
Confirmed independently in pair_0, pair_1, and pair_2 debates
open_questions: How much of current profit levels are deficit-dependent?
Does the channel weaken as deficit composition shifts toward transfers vs. investment?
What is the lag structure between deficit changes and profit impacts?
What is the precise marginal Kalecki multiplier at current deficit levels?
How quickly would corporate profits contract if the deficit narrowed by 2pp GDP?
What proportion of the profit channel flows through interest income vs transfer payments?
